rulings.Case Title: LAURIE BURKE VS. GREENWICH PROPERTIES, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-20-585674, CGC-20-587199, CGC-20-587535 WITH CASE CGC-20-584633
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 14, 2022 line 1. DEFENDANTS' AMENDED MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-20-585674, CGC-20-587199, CGC-20-587535 WITH CASE CGC-20-584633 is GRANTED. Cases involve the same defendants and share significant common questions of law and fact. Cases CGC-20-584633, CGC-20-585674, CGC-20-587199, and CGC-20-587535 are consolidated for all purposes. The lead case is CGC-20-584633. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 14, 2022 line 5. DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is DENIED. Defendant claims portions of the Complaint are false because they are in contradiction with the Governing Documents. rulings.Case Title: UNITY HOMES, INC. VS. GREGORY GEETER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 14, 2022 line 6. DEFENDANT GREGORY GEETER'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend. Plaintiff's UD-101 states that Defendant was served with a 15-day notice to pay rent or quit (Box 7C). This conflicts with Exhibit 2 of Plaintiff's Second Amended Complaint, which shows that Defendant was served with a 15-day notice to quit. Leave to amend is granted for Plaintiff to reconcile UD-101 with the Complaint. Amended form UD-101 may be filed if necessary to comply with this Court's order. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). rulings.Case Title: KIM KLAUSNER, TRUSTEE UNDER THE KIM KLAUSNER VS. AIMEE TSAO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 14, 2022 line 13. DEFENDANTS AIMEE TSAO AND LORIN TSAO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with 5-days leave to amend to allege facts in good faith establishing that as of the time of the service of the operative Ellis Act required noticed Van Buskirk was not a tenant. The Court takes judicial notice of all exhibits submitted by the defendant for the purpose of establishing their existence and their legal effect. The Court also finds that the prior Order and Judgment determined that Van Buskirk was a tenant at all times relevant to the prior action and that any issues relevant to the change of the tenancy per the Trust to a license prior to the hearing in the earlier case should have and could have been raised in that action. The Court does not make any rulings and to the viability of the proposed amendment and any arguments regarding that amendment may be raised later in this action. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: C&C HARGENS, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. JIN PARK ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 14, 2022 line 14. DEFENDANT JIN PARK'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend for plaintiff to allege in good faith that that Plaintiff provided notice to defendant of whether the date of withdrawal was extended to one year and the new date of withdrawal. Demurrer is otherwise overruled, Complaint read as a whole states that there was no written rental agreement. rulings.Case Title: CARITAS MANAGEMENT CORPORATION VS. VERONICA O'CAMPO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Deeming Plaintiff'S Requests For Admissions, Set One Admitted As Truth Of Facts; Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Nichole Santiago
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 14, 2022 line 15. PLAINTIFF CARITAS MANAGEMENT CORPORATION'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR AN ORDER DEEMING PLAINTIFF'S REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS, SET ONE ADMITTED AS TRUTH OF FACTS is GRANTED, subject to opposition. Plaintiff's Request for Admissions, Set One is deemed admitted and conclusively established for purposes of this action. Responses to Plaintiff's Requests for Admissions, Set One were due by February 28, 2022 in accordance with CCP Section 2033.250. As Defendant did not provide responses in this period, the Requests for Admission, Set One are deemed admitted. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303).
